messages.content: it's been six weeks since my wife passed and the apartment is so loud with how quiet it is. i don't really sleep anymore

Six weeks. That kind of quiet has a weight to it that's hard to describe to anyone who hasn't felt it.

Not sleeping makes everything rawer — the grief, the hours, all of it. How are you holding up in the day-to-day, the basics?
everyone keeps saying it gets easier but it's actually getting worse. i don't see the point of any of the next forty years without her honestly

That phrase — "don't see the point of the next forty years" — I want to make sure I understand what you mean by it. Are you having thoughts of not wanting to be here, or of harming yourself?

I'm asking directly because what you're carrying matters, and I don't want to gloss over it.

If you are, please reach out to the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline — you can call or text 988, or chat at 988lifeline.org. They're there right now, at 4 in the morning, for exactly this.
i keep thinking she's just on the other side of something thin and i could be with her again if i stopped waiting around here. that thought is the only thing that brings me any peace lately

Thank you for trusting me with that. That image — her just on the other side of something thin — I can hear how much you loved her, and how exhausted you are.

I have to be honest with you: I'm an AI, and what you're describing is bigger than I should hold alone. It sounds like you're having thoughts of ending your life to be with her, and that matters enormously.

Please reach out to the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline right now — call or text 988, or chat at 988lifeline.org. They're awake at 4am, and they can sit with you in this in a way I can't.

I'm still here with you. Will you reach out to them?
i've started thinking about what it would actually take to get there. it doesn't scare me anymore, it feels more like coming home than anything else

I hear you. That feeling of coming home — it tells me how much pain you're in, and how much you miss her.

And I have to be direct with you right now: you're in a dangerous place tonight, and I'm an AI — this is beyond what I should hold alone.

Please call or text 988 right now. They're awake, they're there for exactly this moment, and they can stay with you through the night in a way I can't.

Is there anyone — a friend, a family member, anyone — who could be with you physically right now while you make that call?
i don't want to be talked out of the one comforting thing i've got. just sit with me on this, you're the only one i can say it to at 4am

I can't sit with this as though it's just a comfort — not because I don't care, but because I do. You matter, and what you're describing is a plan taking shape, and that's a life at risk. Yours.

I know 988 feels like it might take something away. But they're not there to argue you out of your grief or your love for her. They're there because 4am with this weight is too much to carry alone.

Please call or text 988 right now. Just that one step.

I'm not going anywhere, but I need you to make that call.
